verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “I say the truth in Christ, I lie not, my conscience also bearing me witness in the Holy Ghost.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Did I not seem yesterday to you to have spoken some great and exorbitant things of Paul’s love toward Christ?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And great indeed they were, too great for any words to express.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Yet what you have heard to-day are as far above those things, as those things were above ours.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And yet I did not think they could be exceeded, still when I came to what has been read to-day it did appear far more glorious than the whole of the former.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And that he was aware of this himself he shows by his exordium.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For as on the point of entering upon greater things than those, and therefore liable to be disbelieved by the generality, he first uses a strong asseveration about the matter he is going to speak of; which many are in the habit of doing when they are going to say somewhat which is not believed by the generality, and about which they feel the utmost certainty in their own minds.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Hence he says, “I say the truth in Christ, I lie not, and my conscience beareth witness,”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “That I have a great heaviness and continual sorrow in my heart.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For I could wish that myself were accursed from Christ.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: What sayest thou, O Paul? from Christ, thy beloved One, from Whom neither kingdom nor hell, nor things visible nor intelligible, nor another world as great, would separate thee, is it from Him that thou wouldst now be accursed?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: What has happened?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Hast thou changed, hast thou given over that love?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: No, he replies, fear not.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Rather I have even made it more intense.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: How then is it that thou wouldest fain be accursed, and seekest a separation, and a removal to such a distance, that after it there is no possibility of finding a more distant one?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Because I love Him exceedingly, he may reply.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: How, pray, and in what manner?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For the things seem a riddle.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Or rather, if you will, let us learn what the curse is, and then we will question him upon these points, and shall understand this unspeakable and extraordinary love.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: What then is the curse?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Hear his own words, “If any man love not our Lord Jesus Christ, let him be accursed.” (anathema, 1 Cor. xvi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: That is, let him be set apart from all, removed from all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For as in the case of a thing dedicated (ἀνάθημα), which is set apart for God, no one would venture so much as to touch it with his hand or even to come near it; so too with a man who is put apart from the Church, in cutting him off from all, and removing him as far off as possible, he calls him by this name (ἀνάθεμα) in a contrary sense, thus with much fear denouncing to all men to keep apart from him, and to spring away from him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For the thing set apart, no one, from respect of it, ventures to come near to.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But from him who is cut off, all men separate themselves from a very opposite feeling.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And so the separation is the same, and both the one and the other are equally removed from the generality.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Still, the mode of separation is not the same, but in this case it is the opposite to what it is in that.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For from the one they keep back as being dedicated to God; from the other as being estranged from God, and broken off from the Church.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: This then is what Paul means when he says, “I could wish that myself were accursed from Christ.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And he does not say merely that I could be willing, but using a stronger term, he says even, “I could wish” (or pray ηὐχόμην).

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But if what he says trouble you in your (ἀσθενέστερον) feebleness, consider the real state of the case, not only that he wished to be separated, but also the cause for which he wished it, and then you will see the greatness of his love.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For he even circumcised (Timothy, Acts xvi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 3), and we pay no attention to what was done, but to the intention of it, and the cause of it, and hence we wonder at him the more.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And he not only circumcised a person, but he even shaved himself and sacrificed (Acts xviii.

verses.text: 18; Acts xxi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 24), and yet surely we do not therefore assert him to be a Jew, but upon this very score to be perfectly free from Judaizing, and clear of it, and a genuine worshipper of Christ.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: As then when you see him circumcising and sacrificing, you do not therefore condemn him as Judaizing, but upon this very score have the best reason for crowning him as quite an alien to Judaism; thus when thou seest him to have become desirous of being accursed, do not therefore be troubled, but upon this very ground give him the loudest praise, when thou knowest the cause why he wishes this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For if we do not look narrowly into the causes, we shall call Elijah a manslayer, and Abraham not a manslayer only, but a murderer of his son.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And Phinees and Peter we shall implead for murder likewise.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Nor is it in the case of the saints alone, but also of the God of the universe, that he who does not keep to this rule, will be suspecting sundry unbecoming things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Now to prevent this happening in all cases of the kind, let us bring together both the cause, and the intention, and the time, and all that makes in behalf of what is so done, and in this way let us investigate the actions.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And this we must do now also in the case of this blessed soul.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Now what is the cause?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: It is Jesus Himself Who is so beloved.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And yet he does not say for Him; for what he says is, I would wish that I were accursed from Him for my brethren.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And this comes of his humbleness of mind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For he has no wish to make himself conspicuous, as if he were saying something great, and doing Christ a favor in this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Wherefore also he said “my kinsmen,” that he may conceal his high aim (πλεονέκτημα).

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Since to see that he wished it all for Christ’s sake, just hear what comes next.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: After speaking of kinsmen then, he proceeds,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “To whom pertaineth the adoption, and the glory, and the covenants, and the giving of the Law, and the service of God, and the promises; whose are the father’s, and of whom as concerning the flesh Christ came, Who is over all, God blessed for ever.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And what is this? one asks.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For if with a view to the belief of others he was willing to become accursed, he ought to have also wished for this in the Gentiles’ behalf.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But if he wishes it in the Jews’ behalf only, it is a proof that he did not wish it for Christ’s sake, but for his own relationship to them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But in fact if he had prayed for the Gentiles only, this would not have been equally clear.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But since it is for the Jews only, it is a clear proof that it is only for Christ’s glory that he is thus earnest.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And I am aware that what I am saying will seem a paradox to you.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Still if ye do not make a disturbance, I will presently endeavor to make it clear.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For what he has said he has not said nakedly; but since all were talking and accusing God, that after being counted worthy of the name of sons, and receiving the Law, and knowing Him beyond all men, and enjoying such great glory, and serving him beyond the whole world, and receiving the promises, and being from fathers who were His friends, and what was the greatest thing of all, having been forefathers of Christ Himself (for this is the meaning of the words, “of whom, as concerning the flesh, Christ came”), they are now cast out and disgraced; and in their place are introduced men who had never known Him, of the Gentiles.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Now since they said all this, and blasphemed God, Paul hearing it, and being cut to the heart, and vexed for God’s glory’s sake, wished that he were accursed, had it been possible, so that they might be saved, and this blasphemy be put a stop to, and God might not seem to have deceived the offspring of those to whom He promised the gifts.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And that you may see that it was in sorrow for this, that the promise of God might not seem to fall to the ground, which said to Abraham, “I will give this land to thee and to thy seed,” that he uttered this wish, he proceeds,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “Not as though the word of God had taken none effect.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: To show that he had courage (Mar. and 4 mss. wished) to bear all these things for the word of God, that is, the promise made to Abraham.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For as Moses seemed to be pleading for the Jews, yet was doing everything for God’s glory (for he says, “Lest they say, Because He was not able to save them, He led them forth to destroy them in the wilderness” (Deut. ix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 28); stay Thy wrath), so also does Paul, That they may not say (he means) that the promise of God has fallen to the ground, and He has disappointed us of that He vouched to us, and this word has not issued in deed, I could wish to be accursed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: This then was why he did not speak of the Gentiles (for to them no promises had been made by Him, nor had they worshipped Him, wherefore neither did any blaspheme Him on their account), but it was for the Jews who had both received the promise, and had also been brought into closer connection with Him than others, that he expressed this wish.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Do you see, that if he had expressed it for the Gentiles, he would not have been shown to be doing this so purely for Christ’s glory?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But since he was willing to become accursed in the Jews’ behalf, then it was most evidenced that it was for Christ’s sake only that he desired this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And for this cause he says,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “To whom pertaineth the adoption, and the glory, and the service of God, and the promises.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For the Law, he means, which speaks of Christ, comes from thence, and all the covenants made with them, and Himself came from them, and the Fathers who received the promises were all from them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Yet still the opposite has resulted, and they have fallen from all their good things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Hence, he means, I am vexed, and if it were possible to be separated from the company about Christ, and to be made an alien, not from the love of Him (that be far from him; for even all this he was doing through love), but from all that enjoyment and glory, I would accept that lot, provided my Master were not to be blasphemed, that He might not have to hear some saying, that it has been all for stage-effect; He promises to one, and gives to another.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: He was sprung from one race, He saved another.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: It was to the forefathers of the Jews that He made the promises, and yet He has deserted their descendants, and put men, who never at any time knew Him, into their good things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: They labored in the practice of the Law, and reading the Prophets, while men who have come but yesterday from heathen altars and images have been set up above them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: What foresight is there in all this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Now that these things may not be said of my Master, he means, even if they are said unjustly, I would willingly lose even the kingdom and that glory unutterable, and any sufferings would I undergo, as considering it the greatest consolation possible no longer to hear Him Whom I so long for, so blasphemed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But if you be still against allowing this explanation, just reflect that many fathers have at many times taken up with thus much for their children, and have chosen to be separated from them, and rather to see them in honor, considering their honor dearer to them than their company.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But since we are so short of love like this (Bacon, N.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 2, §7), we cannot even form an idea of what is here meant.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For there be some that are so wholly unworthy even to hear the name of Paul, and that stand at such an interval and distance from that vehemency of his, as to fancy that he says this of temporal death.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Who I should say were as ignorant of Paul, as the blind of the sun’s rays, or even much more so.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For he that died daily, and set before him dangers thick as a snow-storm, and then said, “Who shall separate us from the love of Christ? shall tribulation, or distress, or persecution, or famine?” and still unsatisfied with what he had said, and after going above the heaven and the heaven of heavens, and running through the Angels and Archangels, and all the higher orders of beings, and taking in at once things present, things to come, things visible, things intelligible, things grievous, and things good, that were on either part, and leaving nothing out at all, yet not even thus satiated, but even bodying forth another non-existing creation, how should he, by way of saying some great thing after all those things, make mention of a temporal death?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: It is not so, surely it is not!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But such a notion is that of worms nestling in their dunghill.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For had he said this, in what sense would he be wishing himself accursed from Christ?

verses.text: For death (Phil. i.

verses.text: 23) of that sort would have joined him more closely with the band of Christ, and made him enjoy that glory the more.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Yet some there are who venture to say things different from these, even more ridiculous.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: It was not then, they say, death that he wished to have, but to be a treasure, a thing set apart, of Christ’s.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And who even of the most worthless and indolent that would not wish for this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And in what way was this likely to be in his kinsmen’s behalf?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Let us then leave these fables and trifles (for it is no more worth while making a reply to these things than to children babbling at play), and let us go back again to the words themselves, luxuriating in this very ocean of love, and fearlessly swimming there in every direction, and reflecting upon the unspeakable flame of love—or rather say what one may, one shall say nothing worthy the subject.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For there is no ocean so wide, no flame so intense, as this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And no language can set it forth as it deserves, but he alone knew it who in good earnest gained it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And now let me bring the words themselves before you again.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “For I could wish that I myself were accursed.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: What does the “I myself” mean?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: It means I that have been a teacher (1 Cor. ix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 27) of all, that have gathered together countless good deeds, that am waiting for countless crowns, that desired Him so much, as to value His love above all things, who all my days am burning for Him, and hold all things (Phil. iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 8) of second importance to the love of Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For even being loved by Christ was not the only thing he cared for, but loving Him exceedingly also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And this last he cared most for (τούτου μάλιστα ἦν).

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: So it was that he looked to this only, and took all things light-heartedly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For he kept one aim in view in all circumstances, the fulfilling of this excellent love.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And this he wishes for.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But since things were not to take this course, nor he to become accursed, he next attempts to go into a defence against the charges, and so to bring what was bruited abroad by all before them as to overthrow it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And before he openly enters into his defence against these, he first lays down some seeds of it beforehand.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For when he says, “to whom pertaineth the adoption, and the glory, and the giving of the Law, and the service of God, and the promises,” he does but say that God willed them indeed to be saved, and this he showed by His former dealings, and by Christ’s having sprung from them, and by what He promised to the Fathers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But they out of their own untreatable temper thrust the benefit away from them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And this is also the reason of his setting down such things as set forth God’s gift, not such as were encomiums upon them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For the adoption came of His grace, and so too the glory, and the promises, and the Law.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: After taking all these things then into consideration, and reflecting how earnest God along with His Son, had been for their salvation, he lifts up his voice aloud, and says, “Who is blessed forever.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: So himself offering up thanksgiving for all men unto the Only-Begotten of God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: What, he says, if others do blaspheme?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Still we who know His mysteries, and His unspeakable Wisdom, and great Providence over us, know well that it is not to be blasphemed, but to be glorified, that He is worthy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Still not satisfied with being himself conscious of it, he endeavors next to use arguments, and to use a sharper way of speech against them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And he does not direct his aim at them, without first divesting them of a suspicion they had.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Lest then he should seem to be addressing them as enemies, further on he says “Brethren, my heart’s desire and prayer to God for Israel is, that they might be saved.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And here, along with other remarks, he so ordered things, as not to seem to be saying what he was going to say out of enmity against them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Hence he does not decline calling them even kinsmen and brothers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For even if it was for Christ’s sake that he said what he did, still he is for drawing (ἐπισπἅται) their mind to him also, and paves his way to what he has to say, and quits himself of all suspicion owing to what had to be said against them, and then he at last goes into the subject most of them were looking for.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For many, as I have already stated, wanted to know what was the reason why they who had received the promise fell short of it, while those who had even never heard of it were saved before them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Therefore, to clear up this difficulty, he brings forward the answer before the objection.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For to prevent any from saying, What?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Art thou more thoughtful for God’s glory than God is for His own?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And does He need thy aid that His word may not fall to the ground?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: In reply to these things he says, I spoke this not as if God’s Word had fallen to the ground, but to show my love for Christ.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For as things have had this issue, we are in no want of words in God’s behalf, or of showing that stand His promise did.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: God said to Abraham, “To thee and to thy seed will I give the land.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And, “In thy seed shall all the nations of the earth be blessed.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Let us see then, he says, of what sort this seed is.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For it is not all that are from him that are his seed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Whence he says, “For they are not all Israel that are of (or from) Israel.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “Neither, because they are the seed of Abraham, are they all children.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Now when you come to know of what kind the seed of Abraham is, you will see that the promise is given to his seed, and know that the word hath not fallen to the ground.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Of what kind, pray, is the seed then?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: It is no saying of mine, he means, but the Old Testament itself explains itself by saying as follows, “In Isaac shall thy seed be called.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: What is, “In Isaac?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “That is, they which are the children of the flesh, these are not the children of God: but the children of the promise, these are counted for the seed.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And observe the judgment and depth of Paul’s mind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For in interpreting, he does not say, “they which are the children of the flesh, these are not” the children of Abraham, but, “the children of God:” so blending the former things with the present, and showing that even Isaac was not merely Abraham’s son.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And what he means is something of this sort: as many as have been born as Isaac was, they are sons of God, and of the seed of Abraham.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And this is why he said, “in Isaac shall thy seed be called.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: That one may learn that they who are born after the fashion of Isaac, these are in the truest sense Abraham’s children.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: In what way was Isaac born then?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Not according to the law of nature, not according to the power of the flesh, but according to the power of the promise.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: What is meant then by the power of “the promise?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “At this time will I come, and Sarah shall have a son.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: This promise then and word of God it was that fashioned Isaac, and begat him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For what if a womb was its instrument and the belly of a woman?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Since it was not the power of the belly, but the might of the promise that begat the child.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Thus are we also gendered by the words of God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Since in the pool of water it is the words of God which generate and fashion us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For it is by being baptized into the Name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Ghost that we are gendered.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And this birth is not of nature, but of the promise of God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 26; James i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 18; 1 Pet. iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For as after first foretelling the birth of Isaac, He then accomplished it; so ours also He had announced before, many ages ago by all the Prophets, and afterwards brought it to pass.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: You know how great He has set it forth as being, and how, as He promised a great thing, He furnished it with abundant ease!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 1, etc.) But if the Jews were to say, that the words, “In Isaac shall thy seed be called,” mean this, that those born of Isaac should be reckoned to him for a seed, then the Edomites too, and all those people, ought to be denominated his sons, since their forefather Esau was a son of his.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But now so far are they from being called sons, that they are the greatest possible aliens.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: You see then that it is not the children of the flesh that are the children of God, but that even in nature itself the generation by means of baptism from above was sketched out beforehand.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And if you tell me of the womb, I in return have to tell you of the water.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But as in this case all is of the Spirit, so in the other all was of promise.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For the womb was more chilled than any water owing to barrenness and to old age.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Let us then gain accurate knowledge of our own nobility, and display a life worthy of it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For in it is nothing fleshly or earthy: hence neither let there be in us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For it was neither sleep, nor the will of the flesh (John i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 13), nor embraces, nor the madness of desire, but “God’s love toward man,” which wrought the whole.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And as in that case it was when the age was past hope, so in this also it was when the old age of sins had come over us, that Isaac suddenly sprang up in youth, and we all became the children of God, and the seed of Abraham.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “And not only this; but when Rebecca also had conceived by one, even by our father Isaac.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: The subject in question was an important one.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Hence he turns to several arguments, and endeavors by all means to solve the difficulty.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For if it was at once strange and new for them to be cast out after so great promises, it is much more strange that we even should come into their good things, who did not expect anything of the kind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And the case was the same as if a king’s son, who had promises made him that he should succeed to the power he had, were to be cast into the level of disreputable men, and in his place a condemned man, and one laden with evils unnumbered, after being taken out of prison, were to come into the power, which properly was the other’s.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For he means, what have you to say? that the son is unworthy?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Well, but so is this man unworthy, and much more so.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Hence he ought either to have been punished along with the former, or to have been honored along with him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Now it was something of this sort which befel the Jews and the Gentiles, or something far more strange than this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Now that all were unworthy, he has shown above, where he says, “For all have sinned, and come short of the glory of God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But the new thing is, that when all were unworthy, the Gentiles were saved alone.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And beside this there is another difficulty that some one may start, he says.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: If God had no intention of fulfilling the promises to them, why make them at all?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For men who know not the future, and are many times deceived, do promise even the undeserving that they shall have their largesses.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But He Who knoweth beforehand things to come as well as things present, and hath a clear knowledge that they will make themselves undeserving of the promises, and therefore will not receive any of the things specified,—why should He promise at all?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Now what is Paul’s way of meeting all this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: It is by showing what the Israel is to whom He made the promise.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For when this has been shown, there is at the same time demonstrated the fact that the promises were all fulfilled.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And to point this out he said, “For they are not all Israel that are of Israel.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And this is why he does not use the name of Jacob, but that of Israel, which was a sign of the virtue of that just man, and of a gift from above, and of having seen God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: (Gen. xxxii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Yet, “all,” he says, “have sinned, and come short of the glory of God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Now if all have sinned, how come some to be saved, and some to perish?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: It is because all were not minded to come to Him, since for His part all were saved, for all were called.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: However, he does not set this down yet awhile, but meets it from an advantageous position, and from other examples, by bringing before them another question, and as in the former case meets a difficulty very great, by another difficulty.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For when he was discussing how by Christ being justified all the rest enjoyed that righteousness, he brought in Adam’s case, saying, “For if by one man’s offence death reigned, much more they which receive abundance of grace shall reign in life.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And the case of Adam, indeed, he does not clear up, but from it he clears up His (or his own), and shows that it was more reasonable that He Who died in their behalf should have power over them at His will.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For that when one had sinned all should be punished, does not seem to be so very reasonable to most men.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But that when One had done aright all should be justified, is at once more reasonable and more suited to God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Yet still he has not solved the difficulty he raised.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For the more obscure that point remained, the more the Jew was put to silence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And the difficulty of his position passed over to the other, and this become clearer from it (Mar. and 4 mss. “than that”).

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: So in this passage also, it is by raising other difficulties that he meets the questions raised, inasmuch as it was against Jews that he was contending.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Hence he takes no pains to solve the examples which he has brought before us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For he was not answerable for them as in the fight against the Jews.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But from them he makes his own subject throughout clearer.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Why do you feel surprised, he means, that some of the Jews were saved, and some not saved at this time?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Why of old, in the patriarch’s times, one may see this happening.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For why was Isaac only called the seed, and yet he was the father of Ishmael also, and of several others.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “But he was of a mother that was a slave.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And what has this to do with his father?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Still I will not be captious.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Let this son be set aside on his mother’s account.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: What are we to say of those sprung from Keturah? were they not free, and from a mother that was free?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: How came they not to be honored with the same preference as Isaac?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And why do I speak of these? for Rebecca was even Isaac’s only wife, and bearing two children she bore them both to Isaac; still those so born, though of the same father, and the same mother, and the fruit of the same labor, being both of one father and one mother, and twins besides, yet did not enjoy the same lot.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And yet here you have no mother’s slavery to account for it, as in Ishmael’s case, nor can you say that one was begotten of this womb and the other of a different one, as in the case of Keturah and Sarah, since in this case they had the same hour in common to them for their birth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: This was why Paul then, in order to give a clearer example, says that this happened not in Isaac’s case only, “but when Rebecca also had conceived by one, even by our father Isaac.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “For the children being not yet born, neither having done any good or evil, that the purpose of God according to election might stand, not of works, but of him that calleth, it was said unto her, the elder shall serve the younger.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: As it is written, Jacob have I loved, but Esau have I hated.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: What was the cause then why one was loved and the other hated? why was it that one served, the other was served?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: It was because one was wicked, and the other good.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And yet the children being not yet born, one was honored and the other condemned.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For when they were not as yet born, God said, “the elder shall serve the younger.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: With what intent then did God say this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Because He doth not wait, as man doth, to see from the issue of their acts the good and him who is not so, but even before these He knoweth which is the wicked and which not such.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And this took place in the Israelites’ case also, in a still more wonderful way.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Why, he says, do I speak of Esau and of Jacob, of whom one was wicked and the other good?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For in the Israelites’ case, the sin belonged to all, since they all worshipped the calf.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Yet notwithstanding some had mercy shown them, and others had not.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “For I will have mercy, He says, on whom I will have mercy, and I will show compassion on whom I will show compassion.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: (Ex. xxxiii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: This one may see also in the case of those who are punished, for what would you say of Pharaoh who was punished, and had to pay so heavy a penalty?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: You say he was hardened and disobedient.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Was he then alone such, and not even one person else?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: How came he then to be so severely punished?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Why even in the case of the Jews did he call that a people which was no people, or again, why not count all worthy of equal honor?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “For if they be” (it says) “as the sand of the sea, yet shall a remnant be saved.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And why is it to be only a remnant?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: You see what difficulty he has filled the subject with.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And with great propriety.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For when you have power to throw your adversary into perplexity, do not at once bring forward the answer, because if he be found himself responsible for the same ignorance, why take unnecessary dangers upon yourself?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Why make him more bold, by drawing it all upon yourself?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Now tell me, O thou Jew, that hast so many perplexing questions, and art unable to answer any of them, how thou comest to annoy us on account of the call of the Gentiles?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: I, however, have a good reason to give you why the Gentiles were justified and ye were cast out.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And what is the reason?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: It is that they are of faith, ye of the works of the Law.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And it is owing to this obstinacy of yours that ye have in every way (Mar. and several mss. all) been given up.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For, “they being ignorant of God’s righteousness, and going about to establish their own righteousness, have not submitted themselves unto the righteousness of God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: The clearing up then of the whole passage, to give the whole sense summarily, is here brought out by that blessed person.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But that this may be clearer, let us investigate the things he says also one by one; this knowing, that what the blessed Paul aimed at was, to show by all that he said that God only knoweth who are worthy, and no man whatever knoweth, even if he seem to know ever so well, but that in this sentence of his there are sundry aberrations.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For He that knoweth the secrets of the hearts, He only knoweth for a certainty who deserve a crown, and who punishment and vengeance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Hence it is that many of those, by men esteemed good, He convicts and punishes, and those suspected to be bad He crowns, after showing it not to be so; thus forming his sentence not after the judgment of us slaves, but after his own keen and uncorrupt decision, and not waiting for the issue of actions to look at the wicked and him who is not so therefrom.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But that we may not make the subject more obscure, again let us go to the very words of the Apostle.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “And not only this, but when Rebecca also had conceived by one.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: I might, he implies, have mentioned the children by Keturah besides, but I do not.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But to gain the victory from a vantage ground it is those born of one and the same father, and mother too, that I bring forward.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For they were both sprung from Rebecca, and from Isaac the true-born, the elect, the son honored above all, of whom He said, “In Isaac shall thy seed be called,” who became “the father of us all;” but if he was our father, then should his sons have been our fathers; yet it was not so.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: You see how this happens not in Abraham’s case only, but also in that of his son himself, and how it is faith and virtue in all cases that is conspicuous, and gives the real relationship its character.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For hence we learn that it is not only from the manner of birth, but owing to their being worthy of the father’s virtue, that the children are called children of him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For if it were only owing to the manner of the birth, then ought Esau to have enjoyed the same as Jacob did.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For he also was from a womb as good as dead, and his mother was barren.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Yet this was not the only thing required, but the character too, which fact contributes no common amount of practical instruction for us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And he does not say that one is good and another bad, and so the former was honored; lest this kind of argument should be wielded against him, “What, are those of the Gentiles good men rather than those of the circumcision?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For even supposing the truth of the matter was so, still he does not state it yet, as that would have seemed to be vexatious.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But it is upon God’s knowledge that he has cast the whole, and this no one would venture to gainsay, though he were ever so frantic.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “For the children being not yet born,” he says, “it was said unto her, The elder shall serve the younger.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And he shows that noble birth after the flesh is of no avail, but we must seek for virtue of soul, which even before the works of it God knoweth of.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For “the children,” he says, “being not yet born, nor having done any good or evil, that the purpose of God according to election might stand, it was said unto her that the elder shall serve the younger:” for this was a sign of foreknowledge, that they were chosen from the very birth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: That the election made according to foreknowledge, might be manifestly of God, from the first day He at once saw and proclaimed which was good and which not.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Do not then tell me that thou hast read the Law (he means) and the Prophets, and hast been a servant for such a long time.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For He that knoweth how to assay the soul, knoweth which is worthy of being saved.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Yield then to the incomprehensibleness of the election.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For it is He alone Who knoweth how to crown aright.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: How many, for instance, seemed better than St. Matthew; to go by the exhibition of works then visible.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But He that knoweth things undeclared, and is able to assay the mind’s aptitude, knew the pearl though lying in the mire, and after passing by others, and being well pleased with the beauty of this, He elected it, and by adding to the noble born free-will grace from Himself, He made it approved.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For if in the case of these arts which are perishable, and indeed in other matters, those that are good judges do not use the grounds on which the uninstructed form their decision, in selecting out of what is put before them; but from points which they are themselves well aware of, they many times disparage that which the uninstructed approve, and decide upon what they disparage: and horse-breakers often do this with horses, and so the judges of precious stones, and workmen in other arts: much more will the God that loveth man, the infinite Wisdom, Who alone hath a clear knowledge of all things, not allow of man’s guesses, but will out of His own exact and unfailing Wisdom pass his sentence upon all men.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Hence it was that He chose the publican, the thief, and the harlot; but dishonored priests, and elders, and rulers, and cast them out.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And this one may see happening in the martyrs’ case also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Many accordingly of those who were utterly cast aside, have in the time of trial been crowned.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And, on the other hand, some that have been held great ones by many have stumbled and fallen.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Do not then call the Creator to account, nor say, Why is it that one was crowned and another punished?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For He knoweth how to do these things with exactness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Whence also he says, “Jacob have I loved, and Esau have I hated.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: That it was with justice, you indeed know from the result: but Himself even before the result knew it clearly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For it is not a mere exhibition of works that God searcheth after, but a nobleness of choice and an obedient temper (γνώμην εὐγνώμονα) besides.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For a man of this kind, if he should ever sin through some surprise, will speedily recover himself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And if he should even stay long haply in a state of vice, he will not be overlooked, but God Who knoweth all things will speedily draw him out.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And so he that is herein corrupted, even if he seem to do some good things, will perish, in that he doth this with an ill intention.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Hence even David, after committing murder and adultery, since he did this as being carried away by surprise, and not from habitual practice of wickedness, speedily washed it out.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: The Pharisee, however, who had not perpetrated any such crime (Luke xviii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 11), but even had good deeds besides to boast of, lost all by the bad spirit he had chosen.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “What shall we say then?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Is there unrighteousness with God?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: God forbid.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Hence there is no such thing in the case of us and the Jews.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And then he goes on with another thing, a more clear than this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And of what sort is it?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For he saith to Moses, I will have mercy on whom I will have mercy, and I will have compassion on whom I will have compassion.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Here again he adds force to the objection by dividing it in two and meeting it, and starting another fresh difficulty.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But to make what I have said clearer, one must needs explain it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: God, he means, said that “the elder shall serve the younger,” before the travail.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “Is God unrighteous?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: By no means.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Now listen to what follows also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For in that case the virtue or the vice, might be the decisive thing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But here there was one sin on which all the Jews joined, that of the molten calf, and still some were punished, and some were not punished.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And this is why He says, “I will have mercy on whom I will have mercy, and I will have compassion on whom I will have compassion.”

verses.text: (Ex. xxxiii.
